title: Runtime Configuration
description: The thin bootstrap that tells the system what exists in this environment. Four fields, one file, describes the environment not the user. This is the 'desk setup' — it describes the desk, not the person sitting at it. Every field in runtime config is a field that cannot travel with you. Thinner = more portable. Preferences (what you want) live in Your Memory, not here. Source: configuration-spec.md (D144).

memory_root.description: Absolute path to where Your Memory lives on this system. Your Memory is the platform — the defining property of this architecture. This tells the system where to find it on this particular deployment.

provider_adapter.description: Which adapter connects the Engine to AI models. Points to an adapter config file that contains provider-specific settings (base URL, supported models, API key reference). Swapping a provider = swapping this value and providing a new adapter config file. Nothing else in the system changes.

auth_mode.description: How authentication works in this deployment. Architecture default is 'local' (auth on hardware the owner controls). Implementations may define additional modes. Auth is cross-cutting (D60) — independent of the Gateway and Engine.

tool_sources.description: Filesystem paths where the system finds installed tools. At startup, the Engine scans these paths: MCP servers announce their tools via protocol, manifest files are read, built-in tools are loaded from code. Tools self-describe (D146) — config only says where to find them, not what they are.
